response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals with 15+ years of experience across diverse platforms, they lack the specialized expertise required for this embedded networking security role. Most critically, native-level Japanese is a required qualification and is not mentioned on the resume. The candidate's career has focused on web and full-stack development rather than embedded systems, security technologies, and networking protocols. C/embedded experience is limited to a brief 2015 stint at National Instruments, and there is no demonstrated background in Linux kernel work, security implementations (MACsec, 802.1X), or network switch products—all core to this position.
strengths: 15+ years of software development experience with proven ability to work across multiple technology stacks and domains
Strong commitment to software craftsmanship, TDD, CI/CD, and infrastructure knowledge (Docker, Kubernetes, GitLab management)
Open-source contributions and teaching experience demonstrate ability to document complex systems and share technical knowledge
concerns: Native-level Japanese language is a required qualification and is not mentioned; only English, Romanian, and basic German listed
Minimal embedded systems and firmware experience; career focused on web, mobile, and full-stack development rather than hardware-level security
No demonstrated expertise in security technologies (encryption, authentication protocols, MACsec, 802.1X) or networking fundamentals (L2/L3, switch operation, NETCONF, SNMP)

key_factors.factor: No embedded systems, security, or networking domain expertise
Strong general software engineering practices and broad technical foundation
